# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: compute-engine
  type: gcp-types/compute-v1:instances
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: n1-standard-2
    disks:
    - autoDelete: true
      boot: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/ubuntu-os-cloud/global/images/family/ubuntu-1804-lts
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      accessConfigs:
      - name: External NAT
        type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
        natIP: <IP_ADDRESS>
- name: cloud-sql
  type: gcp-types/sqladmin-v1beta4:instances
  properties:
    instanceType: CLOUD_SQL_INSTANCE
    settings:
      tier: db-n1-standard-2
      ipConfiguration:
        ipv4Enabled: true
        privateNetwork: global/networks/default
      databaseFlags:
      - name: log_bin_trust_function_creators
        value: on
- name: cloud-storage
  type: gcp-types/storage-v1:buckets
  properties:
    location: US
    storageClass: STANDARD
- name: load-balancer
  type: gcp-types/compute-v1:targetPools
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    sessionAffinity: NONE
    backends:
    - group: compute-engine
      balancingMode: CONNECTION
